refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and site conditions to ensure testability and context-specificity. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct field trials under representative duty cycle and site conditions; collect telemetry data for ≥100 cycles; compute p95 cycle time; compare against CT_target. Verify that safety-critical functions (e.g., load securing, emergency stop) are not bypassed or degraded in pursuit of cycle time.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual operation time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target (p95).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness that does not degrade cycle time below baseline.
FR-2.3: Safety services shall enforce load securing, stability, and emergency stop without adding non-recoverable delays to the cycle.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, segment times) for performance monitoring and KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic and mechanical systems (pump displacement, valve response, actuator sizing) to minimize load, travel, and dump segment times; tune operator controls for responsiveness.
DP-2b: Implement predictive cycle scheduling and load-balancing logic in the NGHE solution controller to pre-position vehicle and teleoperation resources; minimize idle and transition times.

DP-2c: Design a hierarchical control architecture where the vehicle executes pre-planned motion profiles (load, travel, dump) with minimal real-time intervention; teleoperation provides high-level tasking and monitoring.

DP-2d: Establish a safety-aware cycle time budget allocation: allocate time margins to safety-critical operations (load securing, stability checks) and optimize remaining time for speed-critical segments (travel, dump).
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Implement a multi-layer cycle time optimization strategy combining vehicle hardware tuning (DP-2a), predictive scheduling and load-balancing (DP-2b), hierarchical control with pre-planned motion profiles (DP-2c), and safety-aware time budgeting (DP-2d). The strategy prioritizes safety-critical operations and uses telemetry feedback to continuously refine cycle time performance.

potential_sources: Vehicle hardware tuning (DP-2a) may couple with safety margins if aggressive optimization reduces response time for emergency maneuvers; mitigated by DP-2d safety-aware budgeting.
Teleoperation latency (DP-2c) may couple with cycle time if network delays exceed control responsiveness thresholds; mitigated by pre-planned motion profiles that reduce real-time intervention.
Predictive scheduling (DP-2b) may couple with site variability if load characteristics or ground conditions differ from assumptions; mitigated by telemetry feedback and adaptive re-planning.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP is a composite strategy that addresses cycle time across vehicle, teleoperation, and control layers. Coupling risks are identified and mitigated by design; no fundamental independence violations detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware and teleoperation latency constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, optimization will fail; mitigation: validate CT_target against baseline vehicle performance and network latency early.

Assumption: Site conditions (ground bearing, load variability, operator skill) are representative of duty cycle. Risk: Field performance may differ; mitigation: collect telemetry across diverse sites and refine cycle time model.

Assumption: Safety-critical operations can be time-budgeted without compromising safety margins. Risk: Aggressive time budgeting may create pressure to skip safety checks; mitigation: enforce safety interlocks as hard constraints, not soft targets.

Assumption: Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time control. Risk: Network congestion or latency spikes may degrade cycle time; mitigation: design pre-planned motion profiles to reduce real-time control dependency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2d to explicitly define safety-critical time budgets (e.g., load securing ≥ T_secure, stability check ≥ T_stability) as hard constraints, with optimization applied only to non-safety-critical segments. Add a feedback loop: telemetry → cycle time analysis → control parameter tuning → re-deployment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integrator responsible for orchestrating cycle time optimization across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. It owns the predictive scheduling logic (DP-2b), hierarchical control architecture (DP-2c), and safety-aware time budgeting (DP-2d). It receives tasking inputs, coordinates vehicle and teleoperation execution, collects telemetry, and reports cycle time KPIs.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-level cycle tasking (load location, dump location, load characteristics) from site manager or tele-operator; used to trigger predictive scheduling and motion profile generation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs cycle time telemetry (cycle start/end timestamps, segment times, vehicle state, load weight, site conditions) for performance monitoring, KPI verification, and feedback-driven optimization.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety alerts from Safety Services (e.g., stability warnings, load securing failures); sends cycle time constraints and safety-aware budget allocations to Safety Services for enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via network link; transmits motion profiles, control commands, and receives vehicle state and teleoperation feedback.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of cycle time optimization logic, predictive scheduling, and KPI reporting. It integrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and safety coordination cross module boundaries.

Vehicle owns hardware tuning (DP-2a) and motion execution, but cycle time optimization logic (DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) requires system-level coordination and safety awareness that exceeds vehicle scope. High diffusion: vehicle would need to implement scheduling, safety budgeting, and telemetry aggregation.

Teleoperation owns remote control and latency management, but cycle time optimization requires vehicle coordination and safety integration beyond teleoperation scope. High diffusion: teleoperation would need to implement scheduling and safety budgeting.

Safety Services owns safety-critical constraints and interlocks, but cycle time optimization and scheduling are outside safety scope. High diffusion: safety services would need to implement scheduling and performance monitoring.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because it has system-level visibility into vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services; it can implement predictive scheduling, hierarchical control, and safety-aware time budgeting without diffusion. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services remain focused on their core responsibilities (hardware tuning, remote control, safety enforcement) and receive coordinated tasking and constraints from NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a bottleneck if cycle time optimization logic is too complex or computationally intensive. Mitigation: implement optimization algorithms with bounded latency; use pre-computed motion profiles where possible.

Risk: Coordination between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services may introduce latency or synchronization issues. Mitigation: define clear interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency budgets.

Risk: NGHE Solution may lack real-time responsiveness if implemented in software on a non-real-time platform. Mitigation: allocate cycle time optimization to a real-time or near-real-time execution environment; use asynchronous messaging for non-critical telemetry.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the execution environment and latency budget for NGHE Solution cycle time optimization logic. Define interface contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link with explicit latency and throughput requirements. Specify how motion profiles are pre-computed vs. generated in real-time.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with telemetry monitoring) is directly addressed by the selected DP (multi-layer optimization combining hardware tuning, predictive scheduling, hierarchical control, and safety-aware budgeting). The DP is logically sound and addresses all sub-FRs. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Predictive scheduling logic (DP-2b) is contained in NGHE Solution with minimal diffusion to Vehicle and Teleoperation (via Tasking interface).
Hierarchical control (DP-2c) is split between NGHE Solution (motion profile generation) and Vehicle (execution), but interface is clean (Network Link).
Safety-aware time budgeting (DP-2d) is coordinated between NGHE Solution and Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface; risk of diffusion if safety constraints are not clearly defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner with 95% containment score. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services have clear, bounded responsibilities. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and use existing known interfaces. No new modules or interfaces required.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (Telemetry interface). Field trials can validate p95 cycle time under representative duty cycle and site conditions. Safety compliance can be verified by confirming that safety-critical operations are not bypassed.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-2.1 (vehicle execution) → Vehicle module. FR-2.2 (teleoperation latency) → Teleoperation module. FR-2.3 (safety enforcement) → Safety Services module. FR-2.4 (telemetry reporting) → NGHE Solution module. All sub-FRs are clearly allocated and traceable to modules.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit latency budgets for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces to ensure cycle time optimization is not degraded by communication delays.
Specify the execution environment (real-time OS, cloud, edge) for NGHE Solution cycle time optimization logic and confirm it meets latency requirements.
Document the safety-critical time budgets (e.g., T_secure, T_stability) and confirm they are enforced as hard constraints by Safety Services.
Define the feedback loop for telemetry-driven optimization: how frequently are cycle time metrics analyzed, and how are control parameters updated?

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined, the selected DP is logically sound and addresses cycle time optimization across multiple layers, and the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the optimal owner with minimal diffusion. All gates pass. However, the DP is composite and requires further decomposition to address sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) and to detail the implementation of predictive scheduling, hierarchical control, and safety-aware time budgeting. Decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibilities to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules and to define detailed interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump operations within individual operation time budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target (p95).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote cycle execution with latency and control responsiveness that does not degrade cycle time below baseline.
FR-2.3: Safety services shall enforce load securing, stability, and emergency stop without adding non-recoverable delays to the cycle.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start, end, segment times) for performance monitoring and KPI verification.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware and teleoperation latency constraints.
Site conditions (ground bearing, load variability, operator skill) are representative of duty cycle.
Safety-critical operations can be time-budgeted without compromising safety margins.
Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time control or pre-planned motion profiles.
NGHE Solution can be implemented in a real-time or near-real-time execution environment with bounded latency.
open_risks: CT_target may be unrealistic; validation needed early.
Field performance may differ from duty cycle assumptions; telemetry feedback required.
Aggressive time budgeting may create pressure to skip safety checks; safety interlocks must be hard constraints.
Network latency spikes may degrade cycle time; pre-planned motion profiles reduce dependency on real-time control.
NGHE Solution cycle time optimization logic may become a bottleneck; latency budgets and execution environment must be specified.
evidence_needed: Baseline vehicle cycle time performance (hardware tuning limits).
Teleoperation network latency and bandwidth characteristics.
Safety-critical operation time budgets (load securing, stability check, emergency stop).
Field trial data: ≥100 cycles under representative duty cycle and site conditions.
Confirmation that NGHE Solution execution environment meets latency requirements.
